seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Vercel AI SDK ai coding assistant

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Vercel AI SDK enables developers to create powerful, streaming UIs for code generation, debugging, and repository-aware agents using React Server Components. However, failure modes like partial hallucinations in streaming responses, unvalidated tool calls, inconsistent structured outputs, and edge timeouts can lead to incorrect code suggestions, security vulnerabilities, or credential leaks. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code correctness, scan for vulnerabilities, and ensure safe iterative debugging.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on a Next.js app using Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` and `streamText` for interacting with code-aware LLMs like Anthropic Claude or OpenAI GPT-4. User queries with repository context (files, git diff) trigger an agent loop: LLM generates code → sandbox execution → test validation → iterative fixes. React Server Components stream partial responses to the UI while tools handle sandboxing, vuln scanning, and verification.

Key components include edge runtime API routes for low-latency streaming, structured tool calls for code execution/security checks, and Vercel Blob for repo storage. The agent maintains conversation state across iterations, but risks arise from unverified streaming chunks and provider inconsistencies.

Vex integrates as middleware to wrap `generateText`/`streamText`, injecting verifications for code safety, dependency checks, and license compliance before UI display.
architectureOverview.diagram:
┌─────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐
│   Next.js App   │◄──►│ Vercel AI SDK    │
│ (RSC Streaming) │    │ generateText()    │
└──────────┬──────┘    │ streamText()      │
           │            │ Tool Calls        │
           ▼            └──────┬─────────────┘
┌──────────┴─────────────┐    │
│ Vex Middleware         │◄───┘
│ - Code Verification    │
│ - Vuln Scanning        │
│ - Sandbox Execution    │
└──────────┬─────────────┘
           │
           ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│ LLM Agent Loop  │
│ Repo Context +  │
│ Iterative Debug │
└─────────────────┘

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Incomplete code snippets stream to UI before full generation, showing broken syntax or insecure patterns
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware buffers and validates streaming chunks for syntactic correctness before display

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Sandbox execution or vuln scan results displayed without checking for credential leaks or test failures
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies tool outputs against security and correctness policies before streaming

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLMs format code suggestions differently, breaking UI parsing and validation logic
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes structured outputs and enforces consistent JSON schemas

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Multi-turn debugging with repo analysis exceeds Vercel edge limits, causing incomplete responses
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification offloads heavy checks, preserving edge responsiveness
